# UC-17.3.5 · Posture Assessment Trending

## Description

Endpoint posture compliance rates over time measure security improvement and identify persistent non-compliance areas.

## Value

Endpoint posture compliance rates over time measure security improvement and identify persistent non-compliance areas.

## Implementation

Track posture assessment results over time by check type (AV, encryption, OS patch, firewall). Report on compliance improvement trends. Alert when compliance drops below target. Identify persistent non-compliance patterns.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_zscaler`, Netskope Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 3808), `Splunk_TA_paloalto` (Prisma Access), `Splunk_TA_microsoft-cloudservices` (Entra ID / Intune).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: ZT posture assessment data.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Track posture assessment results over time by check type (AV, encryption, OS patch, firewall). Report on compliance improvement trends. Alert when compliance drops below target. Identify persistent non-compliance patterns.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=zt sourcetype="zt:posture"
| timechart span=1d avg(compliance_pct) as compliance by check_type
```

Understanding this SPL

**Posture Assessment Trending** — Endpoint posture compliance rates over time measure security improvement and identify persistent non-compliance areas.

Documented **Data sources**: ZT posture assessment data. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_zscaler`, Netskope Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 3808), `Splunk_TA_paloalto` (Prisma Access), `Splunk_TA_microsoft-cloudservices` (Entra ID / Intune). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: zt; **sourcetype**: zt:posture. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=zt, sourcetype="zt:posture". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=1d** buckets with a separate series **by check_type** — ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with Netskope CASB and tenant admin for the same users, apps, and time window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (compliance trend by check), Bar chart (compliance by OS), Single value (overall compliance %), Table (non-compliant checks).

## SPL

```spl
index=zt sourcetype="zt:posture"
| timechart span=1d avg(compliance_pct) as compliance by check_type
```

## Visualization

Line chart (compliance trend by check), Bar chart (compliance by OS), Single value (overall compliance %), Table (non-compliant checks).
